notes: Smart planner used 3 wall side(s) for a u-shape kitchen.
Three connected interior-placement walls form a U-shaped kitchen.
Locked refrigerator, locked sink base, and locked L-shaped corner base cabinet are preserved at their provided positions.
The fixed L-shaped corner base at the Wall 1 to Wall 2 corner reserves the first 36 inches of Wall 2 base run.
Preserved 3 locked cabinet/product object(s) from the available walls and designed around them.
Wall 1 includes 2 locked object(s), 0 door opening(s), and 0 window opening(s), so those spans were reserved before new cabinet placement.
Wall 2 includes 1 locked object(s), 0 door opening(s), and 0 window opening(s), so those spans were reserved before new cabinet placement.
Wall 1: Wall 1 carries cooking, refrigerator, and substantial prep-storage runs.
Wall 1: Upper cabinets stop before the refrigerator to keep the tall appliance clear.
Wall 1: skipped base filler because it would overlap an existing object.
Wall 1: skipped refrigerator because it would overlap an existing object.
Wall 1: skipped l-shaped corner base cabinet because it would overlap an existing object.
Wall 2: The first 36 inches are left for the footprint of the locked Wall 1 L-shaped corner base.
Wall 2: A small non-cabinet-sized tolerance remains before the Wall 2 return corner because the locked sink position and wall length do not divide exactly into standard cabinet widths.
Wall 2: skipped sink base cabinet because it would overlap an existing object.
Wall 3: Wall 3 provides a long prep-storage return while respecting the Wall 2 corner footprint.
Wall 3: Remaining end tolerance after the panel is smaller than the available standard cabinet widths.
